seoTitle: How to Use Claude Sonnet for Free: What You Get
metaDescription: Find out how to start using Claude Sonnet without paying, plus what features are actually included on the free tier.
intro: You don't need a paid plan to start exploring Claude Sonnet. This guide covers how to sign up and use Claude Sonnet for free, along with a realistic look at what's included so you know what to expect before you commit to anything. It's aimed at anyone curious about trying Claude Sonnet before deciding whether to upgrade.

steps.heading: Create a free account
steps.body: Sign up for Claude AI with your email to get access to Claude Sonnet without entering any payment details.

steps.heading: Explore the core features
steps.body: Once signed in, try out conversations, document uploads, and other built-in features to see what the free tier actually gives you access to.

steps.heading: Understand the free tier's limits
steps.body: Free accounts come with usage limits, so it helps to know roughly how many messages or how much use you get before you hit a cap.

steps.heading: Apply it to a real task
steps.body: Test Claude Sonnet on an actual task you care about, like drafting content or summarizing a document, rather than just experimenting with sample prompts.

steps.heading: Decide if you need to upgrade
steps.body: If you find yourself hitting usage limits regularly or wanting a more capable model, that's the signal to consider a paid plan instead of staying free.

faq.question: Is Claude Sonnet completely free to use?
faq.answer: Claude Sonnet is available on the free tier with usage limits; heavier or unrestricted use typically requires a paid subscription.

faq.question: Do I need a credit card to sign up for free?
faq.answer: No, creating a free Claude AI account only requires an email address, not payment information.

faq.question: What happens when I hit the free usage limit?
faq.answer: Once you reach your limit, you'll typically need to wait for it to reset or upgrade to a paid plan to keep chatting without interruption.

faq.question: Can businesses use the free version of Claude Sonnet?
faq.answer: Small-scale exploration is fine on the free tier, but businesses with regular, high-volume needs will likely outgrow it quickly.
